briefdescription.para: Power floor for the detector, in linear units.

detaileddescription.para: Substituted for p_avg inside log10() so that a long run of silence yields a large-but-finite measured level (about -300 dB) instead of -INF / NaN. Also keeps the log10() argument a normal (non-denormal) double. Never reached in normal operation — p_avg is seeded with the reference power at create/reset.

agc_steps() runs the detector + loop filter once per chunk of decim samples. decim must stay small relative to the loop time constant ~1/(4*loop_bw); useful values are 8, 16 and 32. 8 keeps the gain trajectory well inside the default loop bandwidth and is one AVX-width vector for the in-chunk gain-apply.

briefdescription.para: Default output clip level (agc_state_t::clip_db), in dB.

detaileddescription.para: 120 dB is a per-component amplitude limit of 10^6 — far above any normally scaled signal, so output clipping is effectively disabled until clip_db is lowered. See agc_state_t::clip_db.

Routes through 2^z = 2^floor(z) * 2^frac with z = v*log2(10): the integer part becomes a raw IEEE-754 exponent and the fractional part a 4th-order Taylor series. Far cheaper than libm pow(); the AGC loop tolerates orders of magnitude more error than this.

briefdescription.para: Fast log10(p) approximation for p > 0 (~1e-3 absolute).

detaileddescription.para: Splits p = m * 2^e via the IEEE-754 fields, takes log2(m) from the atanh series with t = (m-1)/(m+1) in [0, 1/3] (two terms), and scales log2 by log10(2). Used only on the decimated control path, so even the divide is amortised across a decimation chunk.

parameterdescription.para: Loop noise bandwidth, normalised (cycles/sample); ~1/(4*loop_bw) samples to settle. Smaller is slower and smoother.

simplesect.para: Heap-allocated state, or NULL on allocation failure.
gain_db starts at 0 dB (unity gain); p_avg is seeded with the reference power 10^(ref_db/10) so the loop starts settled.
Caller must call agc_destroy() when done.

briefdescription.para: Destroy an AGC instance and release all memory.
Reset the AGC to its post-create state.

detaileddescription.para:
Zeroes gain_db and re-seeds p_avg from the current ref_db. Configuration (ref_db, loop_bw, alpha, decim, clip_db) is unchanged.
state
Must be non-NULL.

briefdescription.para: Process one input sample (exact per-sample control loop).

detaileddescription.para: Applies the current gain, updates the power detector from the gained output, advances the loop filter by one step, then square-clips the returned sample to clip_db. This is the exact reference path; agc_steps() is the faster decimated equivalent.

simplesect.para: Output sample = x * 10^(gain_db/20), square-clipped to clip_db. The clip does not feed the detector.

memberdef.argsstring: (agc_state_t *state, const float complex *input, float complex *output, size_t n)
memberdef.name: agc_steps

briefdescription.para: Process a block of samples (decimated control loop).

detaileddescription.para: Processes input in chunks of state->decim samples. The gain the loop commands is linearly interpolated across each chunk (a first-order hold — no inter-chunk staircase), and the detector and loop filter run once per chunk on the chunk's mean power. Not bit-identical to a per-sample agc_step() loop — see the file header — but equivalent at convergence. Each output sample is then square-clipped to clip_db; the clip is applied after the power sum, so it does not feed the detector.

parameterdescription.para: Output array (length >= n; may alias input in-place).

briefdescription.para: Gain actually applied to the most recent sample, in dB.

detaileddescription.para: Returns 20*log10 of the internal linear gain g_last — the gain the signal last saw. This is distinct from gain_db, which is the gain the loop currently commands:

listitem.para: after agc_step() the two differ, because the loop filter has already advanced past the gain it just applied;
within an agc_steps() block the applied gain is a first-order-hold ramp, and this returns the ramp's endpoint (the last sample's gain).

detaileddescription.para: Use it for telemetry when the AGC is a stage in a larger chain and a downstream consumer needs to know what gain was used, not what the loop last asked for.

simplesect.para: Applied gain in dB; 0.0 dB (unity) at create / reset.

detaileddescription.para: A feedback AGC that drives the average power of its output toward a fixed reference level. Three stages run per sample:

simplesect.title: Loop bandwidth
simplesect.para: The loop filter is a single integrator whose step size is 4*loop_bw. loop_bw is the loop's normalised noise-equivalent bandwidth in cycles/sample: a 1st-order loop with integrator step mu has a noise bandwidth of mu/4, so the knob is expressed as a bandwidth rather than a bare loop gain. Smaller loop_bw is slower and smoother.

simplesect.title: Linear-in-dB
simplesect.para: The control variable gain_db and the detector output are both in decibels, so the closed loop is a linear 1st-order recursion in the dB domain. Because output power (dB) equals input power (dB) plus gain_db, the loop reduces to

detaileddescription.para: which converges to gain_db = ref_db - px_db with a time constant of roughly 1/(4*loop_bw) samples — independent of the absolute signal level. A 60 dB-loud signal and a 0 dB-quiet signal settle in the same number of samples; only a level-dependent loop would not.

Power detector
p_avg is an exponential moving average (1-pole leaky integrator) of the instantaneous output power |y|^2. alpha in (0, 1] sets the detector bandwidth: small alpha smooths hard but reacts slowly.
Topology
Feedback — power is measured AFTER the gain. The gain applied to sample n is computed from samples up to n-1, so the per-sample loop is inherently sequential.
Block processing
agc_step() advances the control loop every sample. agc_steps() decimates it: the detector + loop filter run once per chunk of decim samples (default AGC_DECIM_DEFAULT; typically 8, 16 or 32). The gain the loop commands is linearly interpolated across the chunk — a first-order hold, so the applied gain has no inter-chunk staircase — while the gain-apply and the power sum vectorise. This is sound because the detector average already band-limits the envelope, but it makes agc_steps() not bit-identical to a per-sample agc_step() loop, only equivalent at convergence. The per-block detector and loop coefficients are rescaled from alpha / loop_bw internally, so those keep their per-sample meaning; keep loop_bw well below 1/(4*decim) for loop stability.
Output clipping
Each output sample is square-clipped: the real and imaginary parts are independently limited to +/-10^(clip_db/20) — a square region in the IQ plane, not a circular magnitude limit. The clip is the last operation applied to the output and does NOT feed the power detector: the loop always measures the true, unclipped power, so clipping never disturbs convergence. clip_db defaults to AGC_CLIP_DB_DEFAULT, which is high enough to be effectively off.
Lifecycle: agc_create -> (step / steps / reset)* -> agc_destroy
